mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real sales of final goods and declining housing starts indicates a sectoral rotation away from cyclical demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or fails to predict return direction >55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity, supporting positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize when INDPRO exceeds 2% growth during tightening, or regime-conditional alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.5% triggers immediate risk-off positioning as investors price in delayed monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 over a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll growth above 200,000 signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP and real final sales indicates a broad-based demand shock, forcing equity markets to reprice earnings downward over one month.
failure_condition: The joint contraction signal fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy or interaction coefficient becomes insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase reflects pent-up demand fulfillment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when applied outside the post-shock recovery regime or during normal expansions.
